feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation API - job error logs
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to fetch the error logs for a job.

I have the workflow id, through which I am able to identify the run id (job id), but when I using GET API to pull job status /v1/jobs/{jobId} - API Reference | Workflow | SAP Business Accelerator Hub, it states the job is invalid.

 

I also tried using the jobid from the Monitoring panel, but same error.

 


Please help me the correct way to use the APIs or find the right APIs.

entry.title: What is the Use of Private Environment in SAP Build Process Automation Control Tower?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am curious to understand the purpose of the Private Environment option in SAP Build Process Automation Control Tower.

Currently, we have:

  • Shared Environment
  • Public Environment
  • Private Environment if user activate it

These environments are available while deploying processes. However, when I try to deploy a process, I do not see an option to select the Private Environment.

Because of this, I am confused about the actual use case of the Private Environment in SAP BPA Control Tower.

My questions are:

  1. What is the purpose of a Private Environment in SAP BPA?
  2. In which scenarios is it used?
  3. Why is it not available as a deployment option while deploying processes?
  4. Is it related to agent-based execution, runtime isolation, or tenant-level configuration?

It would be helpful if someone could explain the architecture or practical usage of Private Environments in real projects.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Joule Studio – Cannot reliably access process outputs via prepared_result.prepared_mappings
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m encountering a limitation (possibly a bug) in SAP Build – Joule Studio when working with skills that invoke multiple processes.

Scenario

  • A Joule Skill invokes multiple SAP Build Process Automation processes
  • Each process returns output (e.g. outputMessageX from script tasks)
  • I attempt to directly use outputs in a Message action

Example:

 
prepared_result.prepared_mappings._<generated_id>.outputMessageX.original
 
 

Issue

At runtime, I consistently get:

EL1008E: Property or field '_<generated_id>' cannot be found on object of type 'String'

It appears that:

  • prepared_result.prepared_mappings is treated as a String
  • Auto-generated mapping IDs (_xxxx) are not accessible

Additionally:

  • These IDs are not visible/configurable in Joule Studio
  • They seem to change or are not stable across executions

Current workaround

The only reliable approach is:

  1. Add an Assign Variable step after each process
  2. Map process output → local skill variable
  3. Use the skill variable in the Message action

While this works, it introduces:

  • Additional unnecessary steps
  • Reduced readability
  • More maintenance overhead

Expected behavior

Ideally, Joule should allow:

  • Direct referencing of process outputs in Message actions
  • Stable and accessible mapping paths
  • Or a documented way to reference outputs without intermediate assignments

Question

Is there:

  1. A supported way to directly access process outputs in Message actions?
  2. Documentation on prepared_result.prepared_mappings structure?
  3. A fix or roadmap item for this limitation?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Joule Studio Deployment Error – response_context.value exceeds 50 characters but not configurable
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are encountering a deployment validation issue in SAP Build – Joule Studio related to response_context values.

Error

During deployment/debug, the following error occurs:

$.response_context[x].value: may only be 50 characters long

This leads to failure in Joule compilation:

Compiler - 0190 - Digital assistant project contains validation errors

Context

  • The error originates from generated YAML files under scenarios/*.yaml
  • Specifically, entries like:
     
     
    response_context[n].value
     
  • However:
    • These YAML files are not directly editable in Joule Studio
    • The response_context values appear to be auto-generated by the platform

Observation

From analysis, it seems:

  • The response_context.value is derived from:
    • Process invocation names
    • Action names
    • Internal mapping identifiers
  • Some of these generated values exceed 50 characters, triggering validation errors

Issue

The challenge is:

  • There is no clear way to control or override response_context values
  • Even after:
    • Shortening skill names
    • Reducing descriptions
    • Simplifying output variables

 The error persists

This suggests that:

Internal/generated mapping values may exceed platform-imposed limits without user control

Expected Behavior

  • Joule Studio should:
    • Either automatically truncate or validate generated values
    • Or provide UI-level control over response_context values
  • Deployment should not fail due to internally generated metadata

Questions

  1. What exactly determines response_context.value in Joule Studio?
  2. Is there a supported way to control or override these values?
  3. Are there naming constraints for:
    • Process Automation artifacts?
    • Action names?
    • Process or Skill Inputs, Outputs, Custom Variables Names or Unique Identifiers or Descriptions?
    • Bindings
      that prevent this issue?
  4. Is this a known limitation or bug?

Impact

  • Blocks deployment/debugging entirely
  • Difficult to diagnose due to:
    • Lack of visibility into generated YAML
    • No direct control over the failing field

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: how to setup communication between SAP BPA to Post Transactions to S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

Request to provide suggestion or guidance to establish communication between SAP BPA to S4HANA Public Cloud to Post Transactions BPA processed from Flat Files.

Thanks for the help in advance.

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Prerequisites for Implementing Event-Driven Custom Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Real Estate
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where we need to implement a custom workflow for Real Estate Contract creation, which is not covered by standard workflows.

The expected behavior is:

  • When a Real Estate Contract is created in S/4HANA Cloud
  • A workflow should be automatically triggered
  • The process should go through approval steps in SAP Build Process Automation
  • Then perform follow-up actions (notifications / updates via API)

Based on my understanding, this may require an event-driven architecture using SAP BTP services.

However, I would like to clarify the exact prerequisites and recommended SAP architecture before implementation.

❓ Questions:

  1. What are the mandatory prerequisites to enable Business Events from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for this scenario?
  2. Is scope item 1NN – Business Event Handling required in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Real Estate Contract events?
  3. Is SAP Event Mesh mandatory, or can SAP Build Process Automation directly consume S/4HANA events without Event Mesh?
  4. Are there standard Business Events available for Real Estate Contract creation? If yes, could you please share the event names or documentation?
  5. If no standard event exists, what is the recommended SAP approach (Clean Core compliant) to achieve this requirement?
  6. What are the required BTP services and subscriptions (SAP Build Process Automation, Event Mesh, Integration Suite, etc.)?
  7. Are there specific Communication Arrangements / APIs that must be activated in S/4HANA Cloud?
  8. Are there any SAP best practices or reference architectures for implementing this type of scenario?

🎯 Objective:

The goal is to ensure that we follow SAP recommended architecture (Clean Core + Event-driven approach) and avoid unnecessary custom development inside S/4HANA.

Any guidance, official documentation, or reference architecture would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation – Click Dynamic "Clear" Button Based on Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am developing a SAP Build Process Automation BOT for the Post Incoming Payments Fiori application.

The Open AP/AR Items table contains multiple rows, each with a Journal Entry value and a corresponding Clear button.

My requirement is to find a specific Journal Entry Number (e.g., 40000005) and click the Clear button in the same row.

Since the table rows are generated dynamically in SAP Fiori, I would like to know the recommended approach in SAP BPA to:

Identify a row based on a column value (Journal Entry).
Click a button in the corresponding row.
Handle dynamically generated SAPUI5 table elements.

Screenshot attached for reference.

Any guidance or examples would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP BPA Automation: Web Service Call with authentication
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to call a web service from SAP BPA Automation (without destination) with authentication. So, I first need to call get_token API to get access token and then pass it to next API as bearer token. When I am trying to put two consecutive REST API Call Activity (preceded by Custom script to return options), it is stating if await is missing. I understand that since REST API calls are by default async, it will not wait for the token to be retrieved before moving to next API Call.

I was referring to following doc - Call Web Service Activity | SAP Help Portal. Since I couldn't find Call Web Service activity, I was trying with REST Call Web Service activity.

Please find with the resolution.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: where can I find irpa_core.core.log output in Monitoring of SAP build console?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have added a custom script in the BPA automation. I have added few irpa_core.core.log() statements. I am not able to run the script in Design mode due to firewall issues. While I am running the automation using API Trigger, where can I find the log statements printed? Are they available in Monitoring of SAP build console?

Please help.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Intermittent Issue with Workflow Task Not Moving to Next Approver in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are facing an intermittent issue in our production landscape and would like to know if anyone has experienced something similar.

### System Landscape

* SAP UI5 Custom Application
* SAP Build Process Automation (Workflow)
* Task UI Application
* SAP S/4HANA Backend

### Process Flow

1. User submits a request from a custom SAP UI5 application.?_
2. The application triggers an SAP Build Process Automation workflow.
3. First-level approver approves the task.
4. After approval, our custom logic:

* Updates the request record in SAP S/4HANA with the next approver and approval level.
* Upon successful update, completes/updates the workflow task so that the workflow proceeds to the next approval step.

### Issue

In most cases, the process works as expected.

However, in a small number of cases (approximately 5–6 requests), we observe the following:

* SAP S/4HANA database update is completed successfully.
* Next approver and approval level are correctly updated in the backend.
* Workflow task does not move to the next approver.
* The task remains assigned to the current approver and the workflow does not progress.

### Observations

* Issue is intermittent and difficult to reproduce.
* Not linked to a specific user.
* Not linked to a specific approval level.
* No obvious timing or volume pattern identified.
* Majority of requests are processed successfully.

### Questions

1. Has anyone encountered a similar issue where the backend update succeeds but the workflow task does not complete or move to the next approver?
2. Are there any known race conditions, transaction handling issues, or workflow synchronization issues between SAP Build Process Automation and custom backend updates that could cause this behavior?
3. Which logs or monitoring tools would you recommend checking to identify why the workflow task remains with the current approver?
4. Is there any possibility that the task completion API returns success while the workflow engine does not process the transition correctly?

Any suggestions on troubleshooting approaches, relevant logs, or similar experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

 

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Workflow Management 
@Dan_Wroblewski @Archana

entry.title: SAP BPA Custom Script: Form data
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have an API call with form data. I am not able to pass the parameters correctly. Please suggest how to update the script? (I have masked url and password)

Following is my custom script

const options1 = {
    url: '<url>',
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
    },
    formData: {
    'username': 'auth_user',
    'password': '<password>'
    }
};

entry.title: Joule skill- Error EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map'
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am facing this issue where I am getting the error while testing the Joule skill-
EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map' - maybe not public or not valid?

The skill tester is returning null values but the same when tested from postman works fine. Not sure what is the issue here. Could you please point me to the right direction. 


1.  Test Results

a.

b. API call status

c. Output fields are null

3. Joule skill

Input parameters

Output  parameters

4. The same API is working fine when tested from postman.

Thanks and Regards,
Rudra
Joule Studio, classic edition Joule SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Hi Everyone,I am getting error while executing the package, but my project is working fine.Error
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am getting error while executing the package, but my project is working fine.

Error: A problem occurred while checking the system health (NO_RESPONSE)

The Desktop Agent could not be reached.
I am running Desktop Agent 3 Version 3.44.104

entry.title: SBPA Rules Runtime API returns 403 "bpm.rulesruntime.error" despite valid API key — CAP remote
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm calling the SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) Rules Runtime API from a CAP (Node.js) backend using cds.connect.to() and getting a 403 error even though I'm passing an API key generated from the SBPA Lobby.

{ "code": "bpm.rulesruntime.error", "message": "User does not have sufficient privileges to access the environment." }

const spa = await cds.connect.to("baseline-spa-cc");
const response = await spa.send({
method: "POST",
path: `public/rule/runtime/rest/v2/rule-services?environmentId=shared`,
headers: {
"Content-Type": "application/json",
"Irpa-api-key": "****"
},
body: payload
});

#SPBA

entry.title: Application could not be started due to technical issues. "h.loaded is not a function"
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing an issue with a RAP Fiori Elements application running in SAP Build Work Zone.

Scenario

  1. Open my My Inbox (Integrate with SAP Build Process Automation).
  2. Go back to the Work Zone home (or use the browser Back button).
  3. Open the RAP application.

The application fails to start and shows the following error:

Has anyone experienced a similar issue with SAP Build Work Zone or Launchpad navigation?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Browser extension 'SAP Build Process Automation' not getting installed
entry.content:
I installed 'sap-process-automation-3.32.58' on my laptop but I don't see the Browser extension(SAP Build Process Automation) for chrome or edge installed. I looked into lot of resources and tried all the ways to install the extension but still no success. Need help or clarity on this.

Here's the list of methods I have tried so far,

  • Tried to uninstall and install multiple times to ensure that I didn't miss any steps during desktop agent installation due to which the extension are not getting install.
  • Tried searching for the extension in the chrome/edge extension store, not able to find this extension.
  • Manually configuring the extension on registry.

Also, I am not able to run any test flows that I created even through the agent is connected and added to environment. Need help on this as well.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation flow failing to run
entry.content:
I recently started working on SAP Build Process Automation, there is lot of things I am still figuring out and currently I am stuck with an issue where I am not able to run a simple flow that I created.

I created a new project with just one flow, this flow just contains 1 action (Create Boolean variable). When I run the flow it pops up saying Checking Editor Validity (Screenshot 1) then pops up error saying The project cannot be tested (Screenshots 2 & 3). I have verified that the desktop agent is active and assigned to environment.

Please help me out on why this error is happening and how to resolve it to run flows.

 -> Screenshot 1

 

 -> Screenshot 2

 -> Screenshot 3

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP BPA: Store Credentials for Custom Script
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to call a web service from automation and have added a custom script that returns the request payload consumed by REST Call Web Service Activity. Currently the credentials to get access token are within the custom script. Where can I securely store them,? What is the best practice?

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Process Automation Pricing – Scheduled Report Downloads
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We use SAP Build Process Automation to automate downloading of 11 reports on a schedule and transferring them to another server. The automation runs unattended and is triggered using SBPA's built-in scheduler. All 11 reports are set up as separate applications inside one automation process.

We have the following licenses in our subaccount (Watertec-NonProd):

Automation-unattended plan

We need to understand exactly how usage is measured and billed for this kind of automation. Specifically:


What is the actual unit of consumption for running an unattended automation — is it per execution/run, per minute of runtime, per screen/step performed, or something else?

  • Since our unattended plan has both a RISE (Subscription) and CPEA (Consumption) entitlement source, how do these two interact? Is there a fixed number of runs/minutes included under RISE before it starts drawing from CPEA consumption?
  • If we run this automation daily (11 reports each time), how would that translate into actual consumption/cost under our current entitlements?
  • Does the number of applications/steps within a single automation process affect consumption, or is it purely based on total execution time/runs regardless of how many steps are inside?

    Regards,

    kanakaraj.M

entry.title: Desktop Agent 3 not triggered for "Process and approve your invoices with Build Process Automation"
entry.content:
Hello Teams,

I followed the SAP mission "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" to build an automation to process Invoice, however in the testing process, I lunch the instance, but nothing is happening.

My agent initially was on Mac, but it seems this is not compatible, so I installed a new windows version on a virtual machine on y Mac, but still nothing is triggered when the invoice reached the "Invoices" folder in my Outlook Inbox.

Anyone had similar experience? 
Note that I have already updated all SDK available in the initial project.
SAP Build Process Automation #InvoicesAutomation
